channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: $5,000 Research Grant for African Scholars: CAPSI Adɔyɛ Research Associate Programme 2026 Now Open
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Researchers, academics, PhD candidates, and practitioners across Africa now have an exciting opportunity to secure a $5,000 research grant through the Adɔyɛ Research Associate Programme, an initiative of the Centre on African Philanthropy and Social Investment (CAPSI) at the Wits Business School, University of the Witwatersrand, South Africa. The programme is seeking applications from African […]

The post $5,000 Research Grant for African Scholars: CAPSI Adɔyɛ Research Associate Programme 2026 Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers, academics, PhD candidates, and practitioners across Africa now have an exciting opportunity to secure a $5,000 research grant through the Adɔyɛ Research Associate Programme, an initiative of the Centre on African Philanthropy and Social Investment (CAPSI) at the Wits Business School, University of the Witwatersrand, South Africa.

The programme is seeking applications from African scholars and researchers interested in advancing knowledge on African feminist leadership, women’s philanthropy, social investment, feminist funding models, women-led institutions, and climate philanthropy.

Successful applicants will join a growing network of affiliated researchers contributing to scholarly and policy-oriented knowledge on women, philanthropy, and social investment in Africa while receiving funding to conduct original research.

About the Adɔyɛ Research Associate Programme

Adɔyɛ, meaning “Love in Action,” is a flagship programme of CAPSI dedicated to recognizing, understanding, amplifying, and supporting the role of women in philanthropy across Africa.

The programme builds on CAPSI’s mission to develop a new generation of African researchers, academics, and experts through:

  • Research and publications
  • Academic programmes
  • Knowledge-sharing initiatives
  • Policy engagement
  • Scholarly collaborations
  • Convenings and networking opportunities

Adɔyɛ embraces a broad understanding of women’s philanthropy, including:

  • Everyday and community-based giving
  • Civic and nonprofit engagement
  • High-net-worth philanthropy
  • Professional and academic contributions to philanthropy

The programme also adopts a pan-African perspective that includes the African diaspora and women from diverse backgrounds, experiences, and identities.

Grant Value

Selected Research Associates will receive:

  • A grant of $5,000 USD to conduct their proposed research project

In addition to financial support, researchers will gain access to:

  • Academic collaboration opportunities
  • Research seminars
  • Knowledge-sharing engagements
  • Scholarly networking platforms
  • Visibility through CAPSI’s research ecosystem

Programme Objective

The Adɔyɛ Research Associate Programme seeks to strengthen African scholarship and knowledge production on women, philanthropy, and social investment.

The initiative supports CAPSI’s Research Strategic Plan (2025–2027) and aims to:

  • Expand research on women in African philanthropy
  • Generate policy-relevant evidence
  • Strengthen academic contributions to philanthropy studies
  • Support emerging and established African scholars
  • Advance thought leadership on gender and social investment

Researchers will contribute original work that informs academic debates, public policy, development practice, and philanthropic strategies across Africa.

Research Themes

Applicants must submit research proposals aligned with one of four thematic pillars.

1. African Feminist Leadership, Ethics and Power

This theme explores how African women leaders influence philanthropy, social investment, and development through feminist leadership approaches.

Potential research areas include:

  • Feminist leadership models
  • Governance and accountability
  • Decision-making and resource allocation
  • Power dynamics and institutional transformation
  • Women’s leadership in impact investing
  • Decolonizing philanthropy
  • Digital philanthropy
  • Artificial intelligence and philanthropic governance

Research under this theme should provide practical insights for philanthropic institutions, policymakers, and development practitioners.

2. African Women in Philanthropy, Social Investment and Long-Term Systemic Change

This pillar examines how women’s giving practices and social investment activities contribute to sustainable development and structural transformation.

Potential areas of study include:

  • Community giving models
  • Women’s philanthropic agency
  • Collective solidarity initiatives
  • Impact investing
  • Catalytic funding
  • Social investment for development
  • Women’s contributions to economic inclusion

Researchers are encouraged to investigate how women are shaping more equitable and transformative development pathways across Africa.

3. Feminist Funding and Women-Led Philanthropic Institutions and Social Enterprises

This theme focuses on how funding flows through women-led institutions and organizations.

Areas of interest include:

  • Feminist funding models
  • Resource mobilization
  • Women-led social enterprises
  • Funding ecosystems
  • Access to capital
  • Sustainability models
  • Organizational resilience
  • Regulatory environments
  • Impact investing for women-led enterprises
  • Digital finance innovations

Research should offer actionable recommendations for funders, policymakers, investors, and women-led organizations.

4. Women, Philanthropy, Social Investment and Climate Philanthropy

Climate action and gender equity are increasingly interconnected, making this a particularly relevant area of study.

Potential topics include:

  • Climate philanthropy
  • Climate justice
  • Gender-responsive climate finance
  • Renewable energy investments
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Water security
  • Environmental governance
  • Climate resilience initiatives
  • Women-led climate enterprises
  • Community-based environmental management

Researchers are encouraged to examine how women contribute to climate solutions and environmental sustainability across Africa.

Who Can Apply?

Eligibility is open to African citizens with demonstrated research experience.

Applicants may include:

  • Academic researchers
  • University lecturers
  • PhD candidates
  • Early-career scholars
  • Independent researchers
  • Civil society practitioners
  • Nonprofit professionals
  • Philanthropy experts
  • Social investment specialists

Women are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Requirements

Interested applicants must submit:

1. Research Proposal (2–3 Pages)

The proposal should include:

  • Proposed research title
  • Problem statement
  • Research objectives
  • Methodology
  • Expected contribution

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Applicants should provide a comprehensive CV highlighting their academic and professional experience.

3. Writing or Publication Sample

A sample demonstrating research and writing capabilities must be included.

Expected Deliverables

Successful Research Associates will be required to produce:

1. Peer-Reviewed Journal Article

Researchers must submit one high-quality academic article suitable for publication.

2. Policy Brief or Practitioner Note

A practical output translating research findings into recommendations for policymakers and practitioners.

All outputs must:

  • Demonstrate academic rigor
  • Offer policy relevance
  • Provide practical significance
  • Acknowledge CAPSI as an affiliate institution

Responsibilities of Research Associates

Selected researchers will:

  • Conduct independent, high-quality research
  • Participate in seminars and research engagements
  • Engage in knowledge-sharing activities
  • Contribute to scholarly collaboration
  • Support CAPSI’s research agenda
  • Strengthen African scholarship on philanthropy and social investment

Validation Workshop

Before final submission, researchers will participate in a validation workshop designed to strengthen the quality of commissioned papers.

The workshop will:

  • Facilitate peer review and feedback
  • Encourage scholarly dialogue
  • Strengthen research outputs
  • Promote collaboration among researchers

The event will be conducted in a hybrid format, allowing participation both online and in person to ensure accessibility across Africa and the diaspora.

Important Dates

  • Call Launch: 3 May 2026
  • Application Deadline: 10 July 2026 at 23:59
  • Shortlisting: 17 July 2026
  • Selection Announcement: 21 July 2026
  • First Draft Submission: 1 October 2026
  • Second Draft and Policy Brief Submission: 25 October 2026
  • Final Outputs Due: 10 November 2026

How to Apply

Click here for more about this opportunity

Click here to apply

Why You Should Apply

This programme provides more than funding. It offers an opportunity to contribute to a growing body of African scholarship on gender, philanthropy, social investment, and development.

Researchers will gain financial support, scholarly visibility, networking opportunities, and the chance to influence policy and practice while helping shape important conversations about women’s leadership, philanthropy, and social transformation across Africa.

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post $5,000 Research Grant for African Scholars: CAPSI Adɔyɛ Research Associate Programme 2026 Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026: Win an All-Expenses-Paid Trip to Oxford and Transform Your Climate Innovation into Global Impact
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Are you a high school student with a groundbreaking climate technology idea? Or are you a teacher passionate about inspiring the next generation of environmental innovators? The Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026 presents an extraordinary opportunity for students and educators from around the world to showcase innovative, technology-driven solutions to one of humanity’s […]

The post Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026: Win an All-Expenses-Paid Trip to Oxford and Transform Your Climate Innovation into Global Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a high school student with a groundbreaking climate technology idea? Or are you a teacher passionate about inspiring the next generation of environmental innovators? The Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026 presents an extraordinary opportunity for students and educators from around the world to showcase innovative, technology-driven solutions to one of humanity’s greatest challenges—climate change.

Organized by Saïd Business School, University of Oxford, this prestigious global competition invites high school student teams and teachers to develop practical, creative, and impactful solutions that address pressing climate issues. Participants will have the opportunity to compete on an international stage, gain recognition from one of the world’s leading universities, and win incredible all-expenses-paid prizes, including a trip to Oxford.

Best of all, participation is completely free.

About the Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026

Climate change continues to affect every country and community across the globe. Rising temperatures, extreme weather events, biodiversity loss, food insecurity, water scarcity, and pollution are becoming increasingly urgent challenges that require innovative solutions.

The Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge was created to empower young people and educators to use technology, creativity, and problem-solving skills to develop practical solutions that can create real environmental impact.

The competition also provides an international platform where participants can present their ideas, collaborate with like-minded innovators, and engage with academics, practitioners, and global experts.

In the previous edition, the competition attracted more than 1,600 entries from 75 countries, demonstrating its global reach and reputation.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge offers far more than the opportunity to win prizes.

Participants will benefit from:

  • Free entry with no application fee.
  • Opportunity to compete on a global stage.
  • International recognition from the University of Oxford.
  • Opportunities to develop technology-driven climate solutions.
  • Exposure to leading academics and climate experts.
  • Networking with students, teachers, and innovators worldwide.
  • Strengthened research, teamwork, communication, and problem-solving skills.
  • A certificate of participation for all approved submissions.
  • The chance to present innovative ideas at Oxford.

Competition Themes

Participants are encouraged to develop solutions that use technology to address climate-related challenges.

Possible focus areas include:

  • Air pollution
  • Extreme weather
  • Food security
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Water scarcity
  • Other climate-related challenges affecting local, national, or global communities

Projects should demonstrate how technology can be used to monitor, adapt to, or solve climate-related problems.

Student Team Competition

Student teams are invited to submit innovative climate technology ideas that demonstrate creativity, feasibility, and real-world impact.

Team Requirements

Entries must:

  • Be submitted by teams of 3 to 5 students.
  • Include students aged 15 to 18 years.
  • Individual entries are not accepted.
  • Teams may come from:
    • The same school.
    • Different schools.
    • The same country.
    • Different countries or regions.

Student Submission Requirements

Each student team must submit:

  1. A 10-page PowerPoint presentation, saved as a PDF (preferably under 10 MB).
  2. A video presentation of up to three minutes.
  3. A completed parental or guardian consent form for each participating student.

The presentation should clearly explain:

  • The problem being addressed.
  • The inspiration behind the idea.
  • The proposed technological solution.
  • The expected environmental impact.
  • How the idea could realistically be implemented.

Teacher Competition

Teachers are invited to create lesson plans that inspire students to understand climate change and explore innovative technological solutions.

Teacher Entry Requirements

Teachers must:

  • Apply individually.
  • Submit a series of lesson plans designed for use within their school’s curriculum.
  • Save lesson plans as a PDF (preferably under 10 MB).

Outstanding Prizes

The Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge offers exceptional rewards for both students and teachers.

Student Grand Prize

The winning student team will receive:

  • A full scholarship to the Future Climate Tech Innovators Summer School 2027 in Oxford.
  • An all-expenses-paid experience.
  • Learning opportunities with world-renowned Oxford University academics.

Teacher Grand Prize

The winning teacher will receive:

  • A place on an Oxford Saïd Executive Education Programme in 2027.
  • An all-expenses-paid visit to Oxford.

Finalist Awards

All shortlisted student teams and teachers will receive:

  • An all-expenses-paid trip to Oxford.
  • The opportunity to present their ideas during the global finals.
  • Travel and accommodation fully covered.

Participation Certificates

Every student and teacher whose submission is approved will receive an official Certificate of Participation from Saïd Business School, University of Oxford.

Judging Criteria

Student Teams

Student entries will be evaluated based on:

  • Problem identification.
  • Research and analysis.
  • Innovation and creativity.
  • Feasibility and practicality.
  • Scalability.
  • Quality of presentation.
  • Teamwork and collaboration.
  • Ethical considerations.
  • Sustainability and long-term impact.
  • Overall professionalism and commitment.

Teachers

Teacher submissions will be assessed according to:

  • Educational impact.
  • Curriculum alignment.
  • Innovation and creativity.
  • Clarity and organization.
  • Student engagement.
  • Assessment methods.
  • Technology integration.
  • Ethical considerations.
  • Practicality and replicability.
  • Overall quality.

Research Support

Participants are encouraged to strengthen their submissions through high-quality research.

The competition’s media and knowledge partner, the Financial Times, provides:

  • Climate Capital resources.
  • Climate technology explainers.
  • Trusted journalism.
  • Expert climate analysis.
  • Resources on emerging technologies such as direct air capture, grid-scale batteries, and heat pumps.

Competition Timeline

Entries Open

1 December 2025

Application Deadline

17 August 2026 (11:59 PM GMT)

Judging

International experts from business, healthcare, and climate innovation will review all eligible submissions.

Finalists will be announced in September 2026.

Global Finals

Finalists will travel to the Saïd Business School, University of Oxford, in October 2026 to present their ideas.

Travel and accommodation expenses will be fully covered.

Why This Competition Matters

The climate crisis requires bold thinking, innovative technologies, and collaborative leadership. Through this competition, students and teachers are empowered to become part of the solution by developing ideas that can improve environmental sustainability at local, national, and global levels.

By combining technology, creativity, education, and practical problem-solving, participants contribute to building a cleaner, safer, and more sustainable future for everyone.

Competition Summary

Item Details
Competition Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026
Host Saïd Business School, University of Oxford
Entry Fee Free
Eligibility Student teams (ages 15–18) and individual teachers
Student Team Size 3–5 students
Region Global
Opening Date 1 December 2025
Deadline 17 August 2026 (11:59 PM GMT)
Finals October 2026, Oxford
Finalist Travel Fully funded

Apply

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026 is more than a competition—it is an opportunity to shape the future through innovation, technology, and environmental leadership. Whether you are a student with an ambitious climate solution or a teacher dedicated to inspiring young minds, this global initiative offers an unparalleled platform to showcase your ideas, gain international recognition, and connect with a worldwide community of changemakers.

If you are ready to transform your climate innovation into real-world impact, begin preparing your submission today and submit your entry before the 17 August 2026 (11:59 PM GMT) deadline.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global competition opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge 2026: Win an All-Expenses-Paid Trip to Oxford and Transform Your Climate Innovation into Global Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 1,000 Fully Funded LGF Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers Scholarships 2026/2027 Open for Applications
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Young women aspiring to build careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education now have an outstanding opportunity to pursue university studies with full financial support. The Luigi Giussani Foundation (LGF), through the Leaders in Teaching Uganda (LIT-Uganda) Programme, has officially launched applications for the 2026/2027 Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship. The scholarship programme […]

The post 1,000 Fully Funded LGF Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers Scholarships 2026/2027 Open for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young women aspiring to build careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education now have an outstanding opportunity to pursue university studies with full financial support. The Luigi Giussani Foundation (LGF), through the Leaders in Teaching Uganda (LIT-Uganda) Programme, has officially launched applications for the 2026/2027 Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship.

The scholarship programme aims to support 1,000 young women in Uganda by removing financial barriers and increasing female participation in STEM teaching. Through this initiative, beneficiaries will receive comprehensive support to enable them to complete their Bachelor of Science with Education (BSc-Ed) studies and contribute to transforming education in Uganda.

About the Luigi Giussani Foundation

The Luigi Giussani Foundation (LGF) is an indigenous Ugandan organization that has spent more than two decades addressing educational challenges through research, innovation, and capacity building. The organization is committed to producing responsible, resilient, and self-aware individuals capable of contributing positively to society.

Through the Leaders in Teaching Uganda Programme, LGF seeks to strengthen the quality of education and improve access to opportunities for young women interested in STEM-related teaching careers.

Purpose of the Scholarship Programme

The Female STEM Student Teachers Scholarship is designed to:

  • Increase the number of female STEM teachers in Uganda.
  • Promote access to quality higher education for young women.
  • Empower women to pursue careers in science and mathematics education.
  • Address gender disparities in STEM fields.
  • Support vulnerable groups including refugees and women with disabilities.
  • Strengthen Uganda’s education sector through qualified female teachers.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive scholarship package that includes:

1. Full Tuition and Functional Fees

The scholarship covers tuition and university-related fees for the entire duration of the Bachelor of Science with Education programme.

2. Accommodation Support

Beneficiaries will receive accommodation support throughout their studies, reducing the burden associated with housing costs.

3. Personal Laptop

Each scholar will receive a laptop to support learning, research, assignments, and digital literacy.

4. Academic and Professional Support

Participants will benefit from mentorship and other academic support mechanisms aimed at ensuring success in their studies and future teaching careers.

Priority Groups

The programme emphasizes inclusion and equitable access. Priority consideration will be given to:

  • Young women aged between 18 and 35 years.
  • Refugees and internally displaced persons.
  • Women living with disabilities.
  • Female teachers already working in science, technology, and mathematics who wish to upgrade their qualifications.

In addition:

  • Seven percent of scholarships are reserved for refugees and internally displaced persons.
  • Five percent are reserved for persons with disabilities.
  • Twenty percent are allocated to in-service female science, technology, and mathematics teachers upgrading to a BSc-Ed degree.

Eligible Program

The scholarship supports students pursuing:

Bachelor of Science with Education (BSc-Ed)

The programme prepares future teachers specializing in STEM subjects and equips them with the knowledge and pedagogical skills needed to contribute to Uganda’s education system.

Why This Scholarship Matters

Women remain underrepresented in STEM careers across Africa. By investing in female teachers, the programme seeks to create a ripple effect that inspires more girls to pursue science and mathematics.

The scholarship not only supports individual students but also contributes to:

  • Improving educational outcomes.
  • Increasing female representation in STEM.
  • Enhancing digital literacy among future teachers.
  • Strengthening Uganda’s capacity in science education.
  • Building a more inclusive education system.

Application Process

Interested applicants are encouraged to visit the official scholarship portal and review all application requirements before submitting their applications.

Applicants should prepare all necessary documents and ensure they meet the eligibility criteria.

Application Deadline

Applicants are advised to apply as early as possible. Prospective candidates should regularly monitor the official website for updates regarding application deadlines and requirements.

The LGF Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers Scholarship 2026/2027 represents one of the largest investments in female STEM education in Uganda. With 1,000 fully funded opportunities available, the programme offers a life-changing chance for young women to pursue higher education, develop leadership skills, and contribute to shaping the future of science and mathematics education in the country.

Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY here

The post 1,000 Fully Funded LGF Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers Scholarships 2026/2027 Open for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OSCE Regional Water Hackathon 2026 in Uzbekistan: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Professionals from Central Asia
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Young innovators and professionals from Central Asia have an exciting opportunity to contribute to solving one of the region’s most pressing challenges—water management. The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE), together with regional and international partners, has officially opened applications for the Regional Water Hackathon on Water Management, which will take place on […]

The post OSCE Regional Water Hackathon 2026 in Uzbekistan: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Professionals from Central Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young innovators and professionals from Central Asia have an exciting opportunity to contribute to solving one of the region’s most pressing challenges—water management. The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE), together with regional and international partners, has officially opened applications for the Regional Water Hackathon on Water Management, which will take place on 7–8 September 2026 in Samarkand, Uzbekistan.

The two-day event aims to bring together talented young professionals who are passionate about using technology, innovation, and digital tools to address water-related challenges affecting the region. Selected participants will have their travel, accommodation, and meals fully covered by the organizers.

About the Regional Water Hackathon 2026

Water scarcity and inefficient resource management continue to affect many countries in Central Asia. Recognizing the need for innovative solutions, the OSCE is organizing this hackathon to encourage collaboration among young professionals and promote sustainable water governance.

Participants will explore how technology, automation, digital solutions, and precision tools can help improve water management and enhance resilience in water-stressed environments. They will work on real-world problems and develop practical solutions that can strengthen planning, forecasting, and efficient use of water resources.

The event will be held in the historic city of Samarkand, Uzbekistan, on 7–8 September 2026.

Objectives of the Water Hackathon

The Regional Water Hackathon seeks to:

  1. Foster innovative and practical solutions to water-related challenges in Central Asia.
  2. Provide young professionals with a platform to design and test ideas that can improve water management.
  3. Encourage the application of technology and innovation in sustainable water governance.
  4. Strengthen resilience and promote cooperation among countries in the region.
  5. Support improved planning, forecasting, and resource management.

What Participants Can Expect

Selected participants will benefit from a rich and interactive programme that includes:

Expert-Led Sessions

Participants will engage in presentations and discussions led by experts working in water management and sustainability.

Networking Opportunities

The event will bring together young professionals and specialists from across Central Asia, creating opportunities to exchange ideas and establish professional connections.

Team Collaboration

Participants will work in groups to address real-life water management challenges and co-design practical solutions.

Interactive Activities

The hackathon will feature discussions, group work, and collaborative learning experiences.

Field Visit

Participants will explore practical applications of the water-energy-food nexus through a dedicated field visit.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old.

Nationality

Candidates must be citizens of:

  • Kazakhstan
  • Kyrgyzstan
  • Tajikistan
  • Turkmenistan
  • Uzbekistan

Educational and Professional Background

Applicants should possess educational qualifications or professional experience in areas such as:

  • Water management
  • Food systems
  • Energy
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Climate adaptation
  • Related disciplines

Experience and Skills

Successful applicants should demonstrate:

  • Engagement in relevant fields
  • Motivation to participate actively
  • Excellent teamwork abilities
  • Strong communication skills

Language Requirement

English will be the working language of the event. Therefore, participants should have a good command of written and spoken English.

Application Requirements

Interested applicants are required to submit:

1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

A CV written in English or Russian.

2. Short Essay (Maximum 250 Words)

Applicants must answer the following question:

“In your view, what role can technology and innovation play in addressing water-related challenges in the region? Please provide a brief example.”

Selection Process

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Since selection is competitive, applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early.

Financial Support

One of the biggest advantages of this opportunity is that the OSCE will fully cover the expenses of selected participants, including:

  • International travel costs
  • Accommodation
  • Meals during the event

This makes the programme highly accessible for young professionals across Central Asia.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the Regional Water Hackathon offers numerous benefits:

  • Gain practical experience in solving real-world challenges.
  • Learn from experts and professionals.
  • Expand your professional network across Central Asia.
  • Enhance your knowledge of sustainable water management.
  • Collaborate with peers and innovators.
  • Contribute to building resilient and sustainable communities.
  • Explore how digital technologies can improve resource management.
  • Experience Samarkand, one of Central Asia’s most historic cities.

Application Deadline

28 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

Click here for more information about the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE).

The Regional Water Hackathon 2026 represents a unique opportunity for young professionals in Central Asia to use innovation and technology to address critical water challenges while building networks and gaining practical experience. With full financial support provided by the OSCE, participants will have an excellent platform to develop ideas that can contribute to a more sustainable future for the region.

Discover more global competition opportunities on OFY here

 

The post OSCE Regional Water Hackathon 2026 in Uzbekistan: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Professionals from Central Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Head of Programme Job Opportunity in Kenya: Lead a Multi-Country Entrepreneurship Programme Supporting Over 1,600 Entrepreneurs Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
GrowthAfrica is recruiting an experienced and visionary Head of Programme to lead one of its largest and most impactful entrepreneurship support initiatives operating across Kenya, Rwanda, Uganda, and Ghana. This senior leadership position presents a unique opportunity for an accomplished programme management professional to oversee the strategic direction, implementation, quality assurance, partnerships, grants management, donor […]

The post Head of Programme Job Opportunity in Kenya: Lead a Multi-Country Entrepreneurship Programme Supporting Over 1,600 Entrepreneurs Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
GrowthAfrica is recruiting an experienced and visionary Head of Programme to lead one of its largest and most impactful entrepreneurship support initiatives operating across Kenya, Rwanda, Uganda, and Ghana.

This senior leadership position presents a unique opportunity for an accomplished programme management professional to oversee the strategic direction, implementation, quality assurance, partnerships, grants management, donor engagement, monitoring and evaluation, and operational delivery of a large-scale entrepreneurship programme designed to transform businesses and entrepreneurial ecosystems across Africa.

The programme focuses on supporting aspiring entrepreneurs, innovation teams, and early-stage businesses, with a strong emphasis on empowering young women entrepreneurs in rural and peri-urban communities.

Based in Nairobi, Kenya, the successful candidate will lead a diverse team of professionals while collaborating with partners, investors, mentors, technical experts, ecosystem stakeholders, and funders across four African countries.

About GrowthAfrica

GrowthAfrica is recognized as one of Africa’s leading entrepreneurship support organizations, working to accelerate business growth, strengthen entrepreneurial ecosystems, and create sustainable economic opportunities across the continent.

Through strategic partnerships, business acceleration programmes, capacity-building initiatives, and ecosystem development interventions, GrowthAfrica supports entrepreneurs in building resilient and scalable businesses capable of driving economic transformation.

The Head of Programme role offers an opportunity to join a dynamic leadership team that works across multiple countries, sectors, and stakeholder groups to create lasting impact.

About the Programme

The flagship programme is designed to support entrepreneurship and innovation at scale across four African countries:

  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • Uganda
  • Ghana

The initiative aims to provide support to:

  • More than 1,600 aspiring entrepreneurs
  • 200 innovation teams
  • 80 early-stage businesses

A key objective of the programme is to promote inclusive entrepreneurship by increasing support for young women entrepreneurs, particularly those operating in rural and peri-urban communities where access to entrepreneurial resources and opportunities may be limited.

The programme brings together a wide range of ecosystem actors including:

  • Entrepreneurs
  • Investors
  • Mentors
  • Technical experts
  • Delivery partners
  • Donors
  • Government stakeholders
  • Innovation ecosystem organizations

This collaborative approach is intended to strengthen entrepreneurship ecosystems while fostering sustainable business growth and economic development.

Position Summary

The Head of Programme will provide overall leadership and management of programme operations across all participating countries.

The role carries responsibility for ensuring that programme objectives, deliverables, quality standards, budgets, donor requirements, and impact targets are successfully achieved.

The successful candidate will serve as a strategic leader while also maintaining oversight of day-to-day programme implementation and operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

1. Programme Leadership and Strategic Direction

The Head of Programme will:

  • Lead overall programme strategy and implementation
  • Ensure successful programme delivery across four countries
  • Drive achievement of programme objectives and outcomes
  • Coordinate activities across multiple stakeholders and partners
  • Maintain programme quality and performance standards
  • Support continuous programme improvement and learning

2. Team Leadership and Management

The role includes direct leadership of a team of 24 professionals.

Responsibilities include:

  • Building high-performing teams
  • Managing staff performance
  • Providing coaching and mentorship
  • Supporting professional development
  • Promoting collaboration across countries
  • Strengthening team effectiveness and accountability

3. Partnership Management

The successful candidate will build and maintain relationships with:

  • Development partners
  • Implementing partners
  • Entrepreneur support organizations
  • Investors
  • Mentors
  • Government institutions
  • Ecosystem stakeholders

Strong partnership management will be essential to achieving programme goals and expanding impact.

4. Donor Engagement and Compliance

The Head of Programme will ensure compliance with donor requirements while maintaining strong relationships with funding partners.

Responsibilities include:

  • Donor reporting
  • Compliance management
  • Risk management
  • Grant oversight
  • Performance monitoring
  • Stakeholder communication

5. Budget and Grants Management

Financial oversight is a critical component of the role.

Responsibilities include:

  • Budget planning and monitoring
  • Resource allocation
  • Financial reporting
  • Grant management
  • Expenditure tracking
  • Financial accountability

The successful candidate must demonstrate strong experience managing complex programme budgets and grant-funded initiatives.

6. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL)

The position requires oversight of programme performance measurement and impact assessment.

This includes:

  • Monitoring programme outcomes
  • Measuring impact against targets
  • Reviewing performance indicators
  • Supporting evidence-based decision-making
  • Facilitating organizational learning
  • Ensuring quality reporting

Why This Opportunity Is Significant

This role offers the chance to lead one of GrowthAfrica’s flagship entrepreneurship programmes at a critical stage of growth and impact.

Successful candidates will have the opportunity to:

Lead at Scale

Oversee a programme supporting thousands of entrepreneurs across multiple African markets.

Shape Entrepreneurship Ecosystems

Contribute to strengthening innovation and entrepreneurship ecosystems in East and West Africa.

Drive Women’s Economic Empowerment

Support initiatives focused on increasing opportunities for women entrepreneurs operating in underserved communities.

Work Across Multiple Countries

Gain exposure to programme delivery and stakeholder engagement in:

  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • Uganda
  • Ghana

Join a Leading Organization

Become part of one of Africa’s most respected entrepreneur support organizations.

Influence Strategic Growth

Play a key role in shaping programme strategy, partnerships, learning, and long-term impact.

Who GrowthAfrica Is Looking For

The ideal candidate will possess a combination of strategic leadership, programme management expertise, operational excellence, and stakeholder engagement experience.

Candidates should demonstrate:

Leadership Experience

  • Significant experience managing complex programmes across Africa
  • Experience leading large and diverse teams
  • Strong people management skills

Programme Management Expertise

  • Advanced programme planning and execution capabilities
  • Experience overseeing multi-country initiatives
  • Strong organizational and coordination skills

Donor-Funded Programme Experience

  • Understanding of donor requirements and compliance frameworks
  • Experience managing grant-funded programmes
  • Strong reporting and accountability skills

Partnership Development Skills

  • Experience working with multiple stakeholder groups
  • Ability to manage complex partnerships
  • Excellent relationship-building capabilities

Sector Knowledge

Understanding of:

  • Entrepreneurship development
  • Innovation ecosystems
  • SME growth and support
  • Business acceleration
  • Economic development initiatives

Strategic and Operational Balance

The successful candidate should be able to:

  • Think strategically
  • Make informed decisions
  • Execute effectively
  • Deliver measurable results

This Opportunity Is Ideal For Professionals Who:

  • Enjoy leading teams and managing large programmes
  • Thrive in dynamic and complex environments
  • Are passionate about entrepreneurship development
  • Enjoy working with diverse stakeholders
  • Are motivated by measurable impact
  • Want to contribute to stronger entrepreneurial ecosystems
  • Believe in empowering women entrepreneurs
  • Are committed to sustainable economic development across Africa

Job Location

Nairobi, Kenya

Application Deadline

Deadline: 25 June 2026 at 23:59

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for this exciting leadership opportunity.

Click here for more and to apply

Final Thoughts

The Head of Programme position at GrowthAfrica represents a rare opportunity to lead a transformative entrepreneurship initiative operating across four African countries.

With responsibility for strategy, delivery, partnerships, grants, donor engagement, and team leadership, this role is ideal for an experienced programme leader seeking to create meaningful impact at scale.

Professionals with a passion for entrepreneurship, innovation, women’s economic empowerment, and ecosystem development are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline.

Click here for more global job opportunities

The post Head of Programme Job Opportunity in Kenya: Lead a Multi-Country Entrepreneurship Programme Supporting Over 1,600 Entrepreneurs Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Young Leaders Fellowship 2026 for Emerging Changemakers from the Global South: Fully Funded Leadership Development Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Young Leaders Fellowship 2026, a prestigious one-year leadership development program designed to support young leaders from the Global South who are actively working to prevent violence against children in and through schools. Organized by the Coalition for Good Schools in partnership with Raising Voices, this fellowship seeks to strengthen […]

The post Young Leaders Fellowship 2026 for Emerging Changemakers from the Global South: Fully Funded Leadership Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Young Leaders Fellowship 2026, a prestigious one-year leadership development program designed to support young leaders from the Global South who are actively working to prevent violence against children in and through schools. Organized by the Coalition for Good Schools in partnership with Raising Voices, this fellowship seeks to strengthen the next generation of leaders who are committed to creating safer, more inclusive, and violence-free learning environments for children worldwide.

The fellowship recognizes that violence in schools remains one of the most significant barriers to education, child development, and well-being. Across many countries, children continue to experience various forms of violence, including bullying, corporal punishment, sexual violence, and emotional abuse. Through this fellowship, participants will gain the leadership skills, confidence, networks, and exposure necessary to drive meaningful change within their communities and influence global conversations on child protection and school safety.

About the Young Leaders Fellowship

The Young Leaders Fellowship is a year-long program specifically designed for young individuals who are passionate about ending violence against children in educational settings. The fellowship brings together a cohort of 8–10 emerging leaders from across the Global South, creating a collaborative space where participants can learn from one another, share experiences, and strengthen their impact.

Rather than focusing solely on technical knowledge, the fellowship emphasizes leadership transformation. Fellows are encouraged to reflect on their leadership journey, strengthen their confidence, and develop the skills required to become influential voices in the field of violence prevention and child protection.

The program is built on the belief that effective leadership begins with personal growth. Participants will engage in meaningful discussions, peer learning opportunities, coaching sessions, and exposure to global experts who have dedicated their careers to advancing children’s rights and creating safe schools.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive a wide range of benefits aimed at supporting both their personal leadership development and the growth of their initiatives.

Leadership Development

Participants will engage in regular leadership-focused conversations that encourage self-reflection and personal growth. These sessions are designed to help fellows understand their strengths, navigate challenges, and become more effective leaders within their organizations and communities.

Increased Global Visibility

One of the fellowship’s major advantages is the opportunity to gain recognition as a global voice in the field of school-based violence prevention. Fellows will have opportunities to participate in policy discussions, expert seminars, conferences, and thought leadership activities that influence the future direction of the sector.

Access to Expert Coaching

Fellows will receive coaching and mentorship from experienced practitioners who have worked extensively in violence prevention, child protection, education reform, and leadership development. Participants will analyze their projects, identify areas for improvement, and develop strategies for long-term sustainability and impact.

Continuous Learning Opportunities

The fellowship provides ongoing educational programming that connects practical work with global evidence and research. Fellows will learn from academics, government leaders, civil society experts, and practitioners working in child protection and education.

Training topics may include:

  • Pitching and communication
  • Compliance and governance
  • Recruitment and personnel management
  • Program design and implementation
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Leadership and organizational growth

Field Exposure and Networking

Participants will gain a deeper understanding of the global ecosystem working to prevent violence against children. Through networking events and guided discussions, fellows will build valuable relationships with practitioners, researchers, policymakers, and funders from around the world.

In-Person Convening

During the fellowship year, all fellows will participate in one in-person gathering. This convening will provide opportunities for deeper collaboration, networking, and collective learning among cohort members.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship welcomes applications from young leaders who are already involved in efforts to prevent violence against children in schools or educational settings.

Applicants may be:

  • Founders of non-profit organizations
  • Staff members of NGOs
  • Community organizers
  • Social entrepreneurs
  • Education advocates
  • Government-affiliated practitioners
  • Leaders of school-based initiatives

Importantly, initiatives do not need to be large or highly established. Early-stage ideas and emerging projects are also encouraged, provided applicants demonstrate commitment and clarity of purpose.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Young Leaders Fellowship, applicants must:

  • Be between 18 and 35 years old at the time of application.
  • Be based in and actively working in a Global South country.
  • Be leading or significantly contributing to an initiative focused on preventing violence against children in and through schools.
  • Be able to fully participate in English.
  • Be willing to engage in virtual sessions conducted across different time zones.
  • Be available to attend the fellowship’s in-person convening.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on several key factors:

Demonstrated Commitment

Applicants should show sustained engagement with issues related to violence prevention, child protection, and safe learning environments.

Leadership Potential

The selection committee seeks individuals who demonstrate strong leadership qualities and a clear vision for creating change.

Ability to Influence the Field

Candidates should have the capacity or potential to contribute meaningfully to local, regional, and global conversations about preventing violence in schools.

Openness to Learning

Successful applicants will be curious, collaborative, and willing to learn from peers working in diverse cultural and geographic contexts.

The fellowship will also prioritize equity in geography and gender representation when selecting the final cohort.

About the Organizers

The Young Leaders Fellowship is powered by Raising Voices and the Coalition for Good Schools.

Raising Voices is a Uganda-based organization widely recognized for its groundbreaking work in violence prevention. It is particularly known for developing the Good School Toolkit and the SASA! methodology, both of which have influenced violence prevention initiatives across multiple countries.

The Coalition for Good Schools is a growing global network of practitioners, researchers, advocates, and funders dedicated to ensuring that every child has access to a safe, inclusive, and violence-free educational environment.

Together, these organizations bring decades of experience, evidence-based knowledge, and extensive global networks to support the next generation of leaders.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official fellowship application page provided by the Coalition for Good Schools.

Aspiring leaders who are passionate about creating safer schools and protecting children from violence are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline.

Application Deadline

Applications for the Young Leaders Fellowship 2026 will remain open until 4 July 2026.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Young Leaders Fellowship 2026 for Emerging Changemakers from the Global South: Fully Funded Leadership Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AEYA New Leaders Lab 2026: Fully Funded Leadership, Entrepreneurship, and Community Development Programme for Young Africans Across 19 Countries
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications Open for the First Edition of the AEYA New Leaders Lab 2026 Young people across Africa are increasingly taking the lead in addressing some of the continent’s most pressing social, economic, environmental, and development challenges. Recognizing the need to equip the next generation of changemakers with practical leadership skills, entrepreneurial knowledge, mentorship, and community-building […]

The post AEYA New Leaders Lab 2026: Fully Funded Leadership, Entrepreneurship, and Community Development Programme for Young Africans Across 19 Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the First Edition of the AEYA New Leaders Lab 2026

Young people across Africa are increasingly taking the lead in addressing some of the continent’s most pressing social, economic, environmental, and development challenges. Recognizing the need to equip the next generation of changemakers with practical leadership skills, entrepreneurial knowledge, mentorship, and community-building expertise, the Africa-Europe Youth Academy (AEYA) has launched the first edition of the New Leaders Lab 2026.

Funded by the European Union and implemented through a partnership led by Expertise France, with the participation of the Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), the New Leaders Lab is designed to empower ambitious young Africans who are committed to creating positive change in their communities.

The programme is completely free and provides participants with an opportunity to strengthen their leadership abilities, refine project ideas, collaborate with peers from different countries, and gain practical knowledge that can help transform innovative ideas into impactful initiatives.

Applications are open until 19 July 2026.

What is the AEYA New Leaders Lab?

The New Leaders Lab is a leadership development programme created under the Africa-Europe Youth Academy initiative.

The programme seeks to nurture a new generation of African leaders by providing them with:

  • Leadership development training
  • Entrepreneurship skills
  • Civic engagement knowledge
  • Community development tools
  • Individual mentoring support
  • Peer-learning opportunities
  • Practical workshops
  • Access to a diverse network of young changemakers

The programme lasts approximately three to four months and combines theory with practical application to help participants transform ideas into community-driven projects and sustainable initiatives.

About the Africa-Europe Youth Academy (AEYA)

The Africa-Europe Youth Academy is an initiative funded by the European Union and implemented by a consortium led by Expertise France, together with the Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS).

The initiative focuses on strengthening youth leadership, participation, entrepreneurship, and collaboration between young people from Africa and Europe.

Through innovative programmes such as the New Leaders Lab, AEYA aims to support youth-led solutions that address local and global challenges while promoting inclusive development and sustainable growth.

Why the New Leaders Lab Matters

Across Africa, young people are driving innovation in areas such as:

  • Climate action
  • Social entrepreneurship
  • Gender equality
  • Civic participation
  • Community development
  • Agriculture and rural transformation
  • Sustainable urban development
  • Youth empowerment

However, many talented young leaders lack access to mentorship, training, professional networks, and structured leadership opportunities.

The New Leaders Lab aims to bridge this gap by creating an inclusive platform where young people can develop practical skills, receive guidance from experienced mentors, and collaborate with peers who share a passion for positive social impact.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected participants will benefit from a comprehensive learning journey that includes:

1. Leadership Development

Participants will strengthen their leadership capacity through interactive learning modules designed to improve:

  • Strategic thinking
  • Decision-making
  • Problem-solving
  • Team management
  • Community leadership

2. Entrepreneurship Training

The programme helps participants develop entrepreneurial mindsets and skills necessary for:

  • Building sustainable initiatives
  • Launching social enterprises
  • Creating community impact projects
  • Managing resources effectively

3. Civic Engagement Skills

Participants will learn how to:

  • Engage communities effectively
  • Promote active citizenship
  • Influence local development initiatives
  • Build collaborative partnerships

4. Practical Workshops

Hands-on workshops provide opportunities to:

  • Refine project ideas
  • Develop implementation plans
  • Strengthen communication skills
  • Enhance project management capabilities

5. One-on-One Mentoring

Each participant will have access to mentoring opportunities that support personal and professional growth.

Mentors will help participants:

  • Clarify goals
  • Improve project ideas
  • Build confidence
  • Strengthen leadership skills

6. Peer Learning

Participants will join a diverse community of young leaders from multiple African countries, creating opportunities for:

  • Cross-cultural learning
  • Collaboration
  • Networking
  • Knowledge exchange

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from a wide range of young people, including:

  • Students
  • Young professionals
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Informal business owners
  • Job seekers
  • Community leaders
  • Civil society actors
  • Social innovators
  • Youth advocates

No specific educational qualification is required.

Instead, the programme prioritizes:

  • Motivation
  • Leadership potential
  • Commitment to community development
  • Passion for positive change

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be between 18 and 30 years old
  • Reside in one of the eligible countries
  • Have basic knowledge of English, French, or Portuguese
  • Submit a project or business idea
  • Demonstrate interest in community development and leadership

Eligible Countries

Applications are open to young people residing in the following 19 African countries:

  1. Benin
  2. Burkina Faso
  3. Cameroon
  4. Congo
  5. Côte d’Ivoire
  6. Democratic Republic of the Congo
  7. Ethiopia
  8. Guinea
  9. Kenya
  10. Malawi
  11. Mauritania
  12. Mozambique
  13. Niger
  14. Rwanda
  15. Senegal
  16. Tanzania
  17. Togo
  18. Uganda
  19. Zambia

Project Themes

Applicants must submit a project or business idea connected to one or more of the following areas:

Climate Change

Projects addressing environmental sustainability, resilience, and climate adaptation.

Rural Development

Innovative solutions supporting rural livelihoods and economic development.

Urban Development

Ideas focused on creating sustainable, inclusive, and resilient cities.

Social Inclusion

Projects promoting equality, inclusion, and community empowerment.

Gender Equality

Initiatives advancing opportunities for women and marginalized groups.

Entrepreneurship

Business and social enterprise ideas that contribute to local development.

Community Development

Projects that improve social well-being and community participation.

Commitment to Inclusion

The programme strongly encourages applications from:

  • Young women
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Youth requiring specific support measures
  • Individuals from diverse educational backgrounds
  • Young people with different life experiences

This commitment reflects the programme’s dedication to ensuring equal opportunities and inclusive leadership development.

Application Process

The application process is designed to be simple and accessible.

Key features include:

  • Fully online application
  • Smartphone-friendly platform
  • Available in English, French, and Portuguese
  • Completion time of approximately 10–15 minutes

Applicants can submit their applications through the Capacity4Dev platform.

APPLY HERE

Important Deadline

Application Deadline:
19 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues and ensure successful submission.

Why You Should Apply

The AEYA New Leaders Lab offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Develop leadership skills
  • Access mentorship
  • Join a continental youth network
  • Strengthen entrepreneurial capacity
  • Improve project design skills
  • Gain practical experience
  • Collaborate with peers from multiple countries
  • Create meaningful impact within your community

Whether you are already leading a project or just beginning your leadership journey, this programme can provide valuable tools and support to help turn your vision into action.

Visit HERE for more information about the AEYA New Leaders Lab 2026.

Conclusion

The AEYA New Leaders Lab 2026 represents an exciting opportunity for young Africans to strengthen their leadership potential while developing innovative solutions to challenges facing their communities. Through mentorship, entrepreneurship training, civic engagement learning, and collaboration with peers from across the continent, participants will gain the skills and networks needed to become impactful leaders.

For young people committed to driving positive change through entrepreneurship, social innovation, climate action, community development, or civic engagement, this programme offers a valuable platform to learn, grow, and contribute to Africa’s future. Interested applicants should prepare their project ideas and submit their applications before the 19 July 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Fellowship, Leadership Development and African opportunities on OFY.

The post AEYA New Leaders Lab 2026: Fully Funded Leadership, Entrepreneurship, and Community Development Programme for Young Africans Across 19 Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Aubrey Barker Fund Smaller Grants Programme 2026: Up to £3,000 GBP Available for Surveying and Land Management Education, Capacity Building, Student Support, and Conference Participation in Developing Countries
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Aubrey Barker Fund has officially reopened its highly impactful Smaller Grants Programme, providing financial support for educational, professional, and capacity-building initiatives that strengthen surveying and land management skills across developing countries. Through this programme, the Fund aims to enhance sustainable surveying and land management expertise through professional education, training, institutional development, and practical support. […]

The post Aubrey Barker Fund Smaller Grants Programme 2026: Up to £3,000 GBP Available for Surveying and Land Management Education, Capacity Building, Student Support, and Conference Participation in Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Aubrey Barker Fund has officially reopened its highly impactful Smaller Grants Programme, providing financial support for educational, professional, and capacity-building initiatives that strengthen surveying and land management skills across developing countries.

Through this programme, the Fund aims to enhance sustainable surveying and land management expertise through professional education, training, institutional development, and practical support. The ultimate goal is to create lasting benefits for local communities while strengthening professional standards and knowledge-sharing within the surveying and land management sector.

Organizations, institutions, students, young professionals, and conference organizers may be eligible to access funding through one of four dedicated grant streams, with awards ranging from up to £1,000 GBP to £3,000 GBP depending on the funding category.

About the Aubrey Barker Fund Smaller Grants Programme

The Aubrey Barker Fund is committed to improving sustainable survey and land management skills in developing countries through education, professional development, and capacity-building initiatives.

The Smaller Grants Programme supports projects that contribute directly to:

  • Professional education
  • Curriculum development
  • Institutional strengthening
  • Student advancement
  • Knowledge sharing
  • Capacity building
  • Sustainable land management practices
  • Community development through surveying and land management expertise

The Fund defines Smaller Grants as awards of up to £3,000 GBP and generally releases approved funding upon acceptance of the Letter of Offer.

Successful grantees are required to submit a Monitoring Form by an agreed deadline, usually three months after the completion of the funded activity.

Funding Available

Depending on the grant stream selected, applicants may receive:

  • Up to £3,000 GBP for Capacity Development Grants
  • Up to £3,000 GBP for Bursaries
  • Up to £1,000 GBP for Project Grants
  • Up to £3,000 GBP for Conference Attendance Support

For conference participation grants, a maximum of £1,000 GBP may be allocated to any individual attendee.

Four Funding Streams Available

1. Capacity Development Grants

Capacity Development Grants are designed to strengthen educational and professional institutions within developing countries.

The programme supports:

  • Development of new curricula
  • Improvement of existing curricula
  • Creation of teaching materials
  • Conversion of educational materials into online formats
  • Development of professional institutions
  • Institutional capacity-building projects

The objective is to ensure long-term educational and societal benefits that extend beyond individual beneficiaries.

Who Should Apply?

Applications must be submitted by:

  • Educational institutions
  • Universities
  • Training institutions
  • Professional organizations
  • Professional bodies related to surveying and land management

Funding Amount

Successful applicants may receive:

  • Up to £3,000 GBP

2. Bursaries

The Bursary funding stream supports students from developing countries who wish to pursue educational opportunities that would otherwise be financially inaccessible.

The Fund prioritizes applicants who demonstrate:

  • Significant financial need
  • Strong potential for professional growth
  • Commitment to contributing to their home communities
  • Capacity to create positive impact after completing their studies

Application Requirements

Applications must be submitted by the student and include:

  • Support from an academic supervisor
  • Support from an organization responsible for monitoring expenditure

Funding Amount

Successful applicants may receive:

  • Up to £3,000 GBP

Important Limitation

Only one bursary grant may be awarded per academic year to students attending the same educational institution.

3. Project Grants

Project Grants provide financial assistance for students undertaking academic research and field-based activities.

The funding can be used for:

  • Research equipment
  • Fieldwork expenses
  • Data collection activities
  • Project implementation costs related to academic study

These grants are primarily intended for students studying at:

  • Master’s level
  • Doctoral level
  • Advanced postgraduate programmes

Applicants below Master’s level must present a particularly strong and well-justified case for support.

Important Conditions

  • Applications must be submitted by individual students.
  • Academic supervisor support is required.
  • Monitoring organization support is required.
  • Equipment purchased through the grant becomes the property of the educational institution.

Funding Amount

Successful applicants may receive:

  • Up to £1,000 GBP

4. Support for Conference Attendance

The Conference Attendance funding stream supports participation in conferences and major professional events.

The objective is to increase access to professional networking, knowledge exchange, and industry engagement opportunities for students and young professionals from developing countries.

Eligible Participants

Supported attendees must be:

  • Students of surveying and land management; or
  • Young professionals aged 35 years or younger; or
  • Professionals within 10 years of graduation from a surveying or land management Bachelor’s or Master’s degree

Additionally, participants must be nationals of developing countries.

Who Can Apply?

Applications must be submitted by:

  • Conference organizers
  • Event management organizations

Individual attendees cannot apply directly.

Funding Amount

Available funding includes:

  • Up to £3,000 GBP per application
  • Maximum support of £1,000 GBP per individual attendee

Reporting Requirement

Supported participants must submit reports that are included within the Monitoring Form after the event.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for funding, projects must:

  1. Directly support sustainable surveying and land management skills.
  2. Deliver educational or professional development outcomes.
  3. Benefit local communities.
  4. Contribute to capacity building.
  5. Demonstrate practical feasibility.
  6. Align with the objectives of the Aubrey Barker Fund.

The Fund generally finances actual project costs rather than compensating applicants for their time.

Additionally:

  • Only one active grant may be held by an organization or individual at any given time.
  • Activities must take place within eligible developing countries.
  • Applications must satisfy the requirements of the selected funding stream.

Assessment Criteria

Applications undergo a comprehensive evaluation process.

Mandatory Assessment Areas

Projects must demonstrate:

  1. Community and local benefits
  2. Benefits to the surveying and land management profession
  3. Sustained educational or professional development impact
  4. Promotion of the profession and/or the Aubrey Barker Fund
  5. Good professional practice

Applications scoring zero in any mandatory category will not be considered suitable for funding.

Desirable Criterion

Additional consideration is given to:

  • Projects addressing minority challenges
  • Women professionals
  • Indigenous applicants
  • Minority groups within the surveying and land management profession

Application Review Process

The Trustees follow a structured review process:

Step 1: Completeness Check

Applications are reviewed to ensure all required information has been provided.

If information is missing:

  • Applicants may be contacted
  • Additional documentation may be requested

Step 2: Eligibility and Viability Assessment

Trustees assess:

  • Eligibility requirements
  • Financial feasibility
  • Project credibility
  • Applicant capability
  • Compliance with grant stream requirements

Step 3: Final Scoring

Applications are categorized as:

Category A

Suitable for funding.

Category B

Suitable for funding if resources permit.

Category C

Not suitable for funding.

Category A applications typically proceed directly to funding approval and issuance of a Letter of Offer.

Application Timeline

Applications are accepted throughout the year under all funding streams.

Deadline

There is no fixed application deadline. Applications may be submitted at any time.

However:

  • Applicants should allow up to three months for a decision.
  • The Fund aims to provide decisions within six weeks whenever possible.

 

Click here for more and to apply

Monitoring and Reporting

Successful applicants are required to:

  • Complete funded activities as proposed
  • Maintain communication with the Trustees
  • Submit a Monitoring Form by the agreed reporting date

Typically, Monitoring Forms are due approximately three months after project completion.

Failure to submit the Monitoring Form may result in ineligibility for future Aubrey Barker Fund support.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Aubrey Barker Fund Smaller Grants Programme provides an important source of support for professionals, students, institutions, and organizations working to improve surveying and land management capabilities in developing countries.

By supporting education, institutional development, research, professional engagement, and community-focused initiatives, the programme contributes to stronger professional capacity, improved land management practices, and long-term sustainable development outcomes.

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post Aubrey Barker Fund Smaller Grants Programme 2026: Up to £3,000 GBP Available for Surveying and Land Management Education, Capacity Building, Student Support, and Conference Participation in Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma 2026–2027: Fully Funded Leadership and Recovery Programme for Syrian Professionals
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma 2026–2027 is a specialised professional education opportunity for Syrian professionals who are contributing to governance, development, institutional reform, social cohesion, recovery and resilience efforts in Syria. Delivered by the Geneva Graduate Institute Executive Education, the programme is known as the Development Policies and Practices Syria – Governance, Resilience […]

The post Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma 2026–2027: Fully Funded Leadership and Recovery Programme for Syrian Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma 2026–2027 is a specialised professional education opportunity for Syrian professionals who are contributing to governance, development, institutional reform, social cohesion, recovery and resilience efforts in Syria.

Delivered by the Geneva Graduate Institute Executive Education, the programme is known as the Development Policies and Practices Syria – Governance, Resilience and Recovery programme, also called DPP–GRR. It has been designed to support a new generation of Syrian professionals who are prepared to take on leadership roles in institutions, communities, organisations and initiatives that contribute to inclusive and sustainable recovery in post-conflict settings.

The programme combines academic learning, practical workplace-based research, leadership development and international exposure. Participants will take part in an online component, an intensive in-person learning module in Damascus, a workplace-based learning phase, and a final executive module in Geneva, Switzerland.

Applications for the 2026–2027 cohort are currently open. The application deadline is 22 June 2026.

About the Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma

The Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma is an advanced executive education programme focused on the realities of governance and recovery in fragile and conflict-affected environments. It is particularly relevant for professionals who are already working, or who aspire to work, in areas such as public service delivery, institutional strengthening, humanitarian and development programming, community recovery, policy development, social cohesion, local economic development and peacebuilding.

The programme recognises that recovery is not only about rebuilding infrastructure. Sustainable recovery also requires effective institutions, accountable governance, inclusive decision-making, social trust, local leadership, economic opportunity and collaboration between public institutions, civil society, academia, development actors and the private sector.

Through this diploma, participants will build the knowledge, leadership capacity and practical tools needed to contribute meaningfully to recovery processes in Syria. The programme places strong emphasis on applied learning. Rather than studying governance and recovery only in theory, participants are encouraged to connect their learning directly to challenges within their institutions, communities and professional sectors.

Successful participants will receive an Executive Diploma in Development Policies and Practices – Governance, Resilience and Recovery, equivalent to a Diploma of Advanced Studies worth 30 ECTS credits.

Why the Governance Resilience and Recovery Programme Matters

Governance plays a central role in rebuilding societies affected by conflict and instability. Strong institutions, inclusive leadership, accountable systems and citizen-centred services are essential to improving trust, supporting development and helping communities recover.

The DPP–GRR programme equips participants to understand and respond to these complex realities. It focuses on governance, resilience, development, institutional recovery, human security, social cohesion and inclusive service delivery. Participants will also strengthen key transferable skills such as critical thinking, strategic decision-making, analysis, negotiation, problem-solving and leadership in complex environments.

The programme is particularly valuable for professionals who want to move from operational roles into stronger leadership and policy-oriented positions. It offers an opportunity to develop practical expertise while building a network of peers, mentors, academics and policy practitioners working in related fields.

Benefits of the Executive Diploma Programme

Eligible participants selected for the programme will receive full scholarships. These scholarships cover academic costs, mentoring, participation in the Geneva module and programme-related activities through institutional funding and partnerships.

Limited travel and accommodation support may also be available for eligible participants during the in-person modules.

Beyond financial support, participants benefit from a structured professional development experience that includes academic instruction, practical research, leadership training and international engagement.

Key benefits of the programme include:

  • Full scholarship support for selected eligible participants.
  • An Executive Diploma in Development Policies and Practices – Governance, Resilience and Recovery.
  • A Diploma of Advanced Studies worth 30 ECTS credits.
  • Online, in-person and workplace-based learning opportunities.
  • An intensive regional learning module in Damascus, Syria.
  • Professional mentoring and academic supervision.
  • Access to specialised online thematic courses.
  • The opportunity to develop a professional thesis based on a real governance, resilience or recovery challenge.
  • Leadership, negotiation and strategic management training in Geneva.
  • Interaction with international organisations, Swiss institutions and Geneva-based policy actors.
  • Access to professional networks, peer learning and policy dialogues.
  • Membership in the wider Development Policies and Practices and Conflict and Fragility Management alumni networks.

Participants will join a wider international community of more than 1,250 professionals working in governance, peacebuilding, development and humanitarian action.

Programme Structure and Learning Modules

The Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma is delivered through three major learning modules.

Module 1: Online Learning and Damascus Regional Hub

The programme begins with an online introductory component developed in collaboration with Damascus University. This is followed by an intensive in-person module in Damascus at the Syrian Diplomatic Institute.

The first module focuses on major themes including governance, development, institutional recovery, resilience, social cohesion, human security and inclusive service delivery in post-conflict and complex settings.

Participants will also build analytical and methodological skills. These include critical thinking, strategic planning, problem-solving and decision-making in environments where institutions and communities may be navigating uncertainty and change.

The online component is scheduled for 3–7 August 2026, while the in-person Damascus component will take place from 11–27 August 2026.

Module 2: Workplace-Based Learning

From September to November 2026, participants will return to their workplaces and professional environments for a practice-based learning period.
