During this phase, each participant will develop a professional thesis or applied research project connected to a real challenge in governance, resilience or recovery. The project may focus on an issue within an institution, organisation, local administration, community or professional sector.

Participants will receive mentoring, academic supervision and access to specialised online learning resources throughout this period. This approach ensures that the programme produces learning that is relevant, practical and directly connected to participants’ professional responsibilities.

Module 3: Geneva Leadership and International Policy Engagement

The final module will take place in Geneva, Switzerland, from 11–22 January 2027.

This part of the programme focuses on leadership, negotiation, strategic management and international policy engagement. Participants will present and defend their professional papers while engaging with international organisations, Swiss institutions and policy actors based in Geneva.

The Geneva module provides a valuable international perspective on governance, development, recovery and institutional cooperation. It also gives participants the chance to connect their Syria-focused professional work with broader global conversations on peacebuilding, development, humanitarian action and resilience.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is intended for Syrian professionals who are committed to contributing to recovery, resilience and institutional development in Syria.

It is suitable for professionals working in public institutions, local administrations, international organisations, non-governmental organisations, academic institutions, civil society organisations and the private sector.

Applicants may be working in areas such as public administration, governance, development, humanitarian programming, policy development, social cohesion, community-based recovery, local economic development, entrepreneurship, institutional reform and sustainable development.

The programme is also suitable for professionals who aspire to take on leadership responsibilities in these sectors and want to gain stronger practical and policy-oriented expertise.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a Syrian national residing in Syria.
  • Hold a university degree or demonstrate equivalent professional experience.
  • Have relevant experience in governance, development, recovery, resilience, public administration, civil society, academia or the private sector.
  • Demonstrate leadership potential and engagement with an institution, organisation or professional sector.
  • Be able to commit to the full duration of the programme, including online learning, the Damascus module, workplace-based learning and the Geneva module.
  • Demonstrate proficiency in English.

The selection process also gives attention to diversity, including gender diversity, professional background, sectoral experience and regional representation.

Application Process

Applications are submitted through a competitive selection process coordinated by the Geneva Graduate Institute in collaboration with programme partners and relevant stakeholders.

Applicants should be prepared to submit an online application together with supporting documents. The process generally includes a curriculum vitae, a motivation letter, professional and academic references, and a preliminary professional thesis or research topic related to governance, resilience or recovery.

Shortlisted applicants may also be invited for an interview and selection review process.

Applicants should ensure that their proposed research or professional thesis topic is connected to a real issue they are interested in addressing through their work, institution or community.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma 2026–2027 is 22 June 2026.

Interested applicants should begin preparing their documents early, especially their CV, motivation letter, references and preliminary professional thesis or research topic.

How to Apply

Applicants can access the official programme page, review the full details and begin the application process through the Geneva Graduate Institute Executive Education website.

 

Final Thoughts

The Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma offers a meaningful opportunity for Syrian professionals who want to strengthen their leadership skills, expand their professional networks and contribute to inclusive recovery in Syria.

With full scholarship support for eligible selected participants, a practical workplace-based thesis, learning opportunities in Damascus and Geneva, and access to an international alumni community, the programme is designed to support professionals who are ready to turn knowledge into action.

Applicants with experience or interest in governance, public administration, civil society, development, resilience, recovery, entrepreneurship and institutional reform should consider applying before the 22 June 2026 deadline.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here. 

The post Governance Resilience and Recovery Executive Diploma 2026–2027: Fully Funded Leadership and Recovery Programme for Syrian Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy 2026: Apply for a Transformative Leadership Programme for Democratic Change in Europe
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy (DILA) 2026 is now accepting applications from mid-career public leaders who are committed to strengthening democracy, improving governance and driving meaningful change in their communities, countries and across Europe. Hosted by the Democracy Institute at Central European University, DILA is a three-month, part-time leadership development programme designed for a […]

The post CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy 2026: Apply for a Transformative Leadership Programme for Democratic Change in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy (DILA) 2026 is now accepting applications from mid-career public leaders who are committed to strengthening democracy, improving governance and driving meaningful change in their communities, countries and across Europe.

Hosted by the Democracy Institute at Central European University, DILA is a three-month, part-time leadership development programme designed for a new generation of leaders working at the intersection of public service, politics, civil society, policy and democratic governance.

As democratic institutions, public trust and civic participation continue to face significant challenges in many parts of Europe, there is a growing need for leaders who can think strategically, work across differences, communicate effectively and create practical solutions for their communities. The Democracy Institute Leadership Academy responds to this need by bringing together public leaders, policy experts, political representatives and civil society actors from Central Europe, Southern Europe and the Western Balkans.

The programme provides participants with an opportunity to learn from international scholars, experienced practitioners and fellow leaders who are actively engaged in democratic life. Through a combination of residential learning sessions in Budapest and interactive online sessions, fellows will explore the realities of democratic governance and develop the leadership tools needed to respond to complex public challenges.

What Is the CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy?

The Democracy Institute Leadership Academy, widely known as DILA, is an English-language leadership training programme offered by the CEU Democracy Institute. It is structured as a three-month, part-time programme, making it suitable for professionals who are already working in demanding public, political, policy or civil society roles.

DILA is not simply a classroom-based programme. It is designed as a practical leadership experience that connects theory with real-life public challenges. Participants are expected to engage with pressing questions about democracy, political leadership, public policy, civic participation and institutional resilience.

The programme focuses on the realities facing democratic societies today. Fellows will have the opportunity to examine challenges affecting local communities, national governments, political parties, civil society organisations and the wider European democratic space.

The academy also creates a space for participants to exchange ideas with people from different countries, professional backgrounds and political experiences. This peer-learning environment is one of the programme’s most valuable features because it allows fellows to learn from the successes, challenges and perspectives of others working to strengthen democratic life in their own contexts.

Why the DILA 2026 Programme Matters

Democracy depends on informed, courageous and capable leaders. However, leadership in public life requires more than good intentions. It requires strategic thinking, the ability to build trust, strong communication skills, a clear understanding of institutions and policies, and the capacity to work with people who may have different views or interests.

The DILA 2026 programme is designed to help participants build these capacities.

Participants will explore democratic governance at local, national and European levels. They will examine how public leaders can respond to real challenges while remaining committed to democratic values, accountability, participation and inclusion.

The academy is particularly relevant for leaders who want to improve their ability to develop and communicate ideas, lead teams, engage communities, influence policy and contribute to democratic change.

By joining DILA, fellows become part of a wider network of leaders who are committed to public service and democratic resilience. This network can create opportunities for future collaboration, knowledge-sharing and cross-border engagement long after the three-month programme has ended.

What Participants Can Expect From the Programme

The CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy 2026 will be delivered through a hybrid learning format. This means that participants will take part in both in-person and online learning activities.

The programme includes three four-day residential sessions in Budapest, Hungary, at the CEU Democracy Institute. These sessions offer fellows an opportunity to participate in intensive discussions, workshops, networking activities and collaborative learning with other participants and faculty members.

Between the residential sessions, participants will join weekly interactive online meetings. These online sessions are scheduled for Thursday mornings and are designed to help fellows remain engaged with the programme while continuing their professional responsibilities.

The hybrid structure allows participants to benefit from in-person learning and international networking while also maintaining a connection to their ongoing work, public responsibilities and communities.

The programme will be conducted in English and will feature international scholars and experienced practitioners with expertise in democracy, governance, public policy and leadership.

Key Learning Areas at DILA 2026

The DILA 2026 programme addresses some of the most important issues facing democratic leaders today. While participants will bring their own professional experiences and country contexts into the learning process, the programme is expected to focus on areas such as democratic governance, strategic thinking, public leadership and policy challenges.

Fellows will gain exposure to current debates about democracy in Europe and will be encouraged to think critically about the role of leaders in protecting democratic institutions and expanding public participation.

The programme also supports participants in developing leadership and strategic skills that are useful for careers in politics, public administration, civil society, policy advocacy, think tanks and community leadership.

Participants can expect to strengthen their ability to analyse public challenges, communicate ideas clearly, collaborate with diverse stakeholders and contribute to practical democratic solutions.

Who Should Consider Applying?

DILA 2026 is intended for mid-career professionals who are already involved in public life and want to deepen their leadership capacity.

The programme is especially relevant for local, municipal and national leaders; political party representatives; civil society activists; policy experts; and professionals working in public institutions, think tanks or related organisations.

Applicants should have a strong interest in democratic governance and a clear commitment to promoting democratic change. They should also be prepared to actively participate in a rigorous, collaborative and English-language learning environment.

This is an excellent opportunity for public leaders who want to expand their regional network, learn from international experts and gain practical tools for navigating the complex realities of democratic leadership.

How to Apply for DILA 2026

Interested applicants should visit the official CEU Democracy Institute DILA 2026 call page for complete application instructions, requirements and access to the application process.

Applicants are encouraged to review the official information carefully and prepare their materials early. Since the programme brings together a select group of mid-career leaders from across Central Europe, Southern Europe and the Western Balkans, prospective fellows should ensure that their application clearly demonstrates their leadership experience, public engagement and commitment to democratic values.

The application deadline for the CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy 2026 is July 5, 2026. Applicants should submit their materials before the deadline because late applications may not be considered.

Final Thoughts

The CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy 2026 is a valuable opportunity for public leaders who want to make a stronger contribution to democratic governance and public life.

Through its hybrid format, international faculty, peer-learning environment and focus on practical leadership, DILA offers participants more than a short training experience. It offers a platform for learning, reflection, collaboration and long-term professional growth.

For leaders working to strengthen democracy in Central Europe, Southern Europe and the Western Balkans, DILA 2026 provides an opportunity to connect with a community of peers who share a commitment to democratic change.

Application Deadline: July 5, 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here .

For more global opportunities, click here. 

The post CEU Democracy Institute Leadership Academy 2026: Apply for a Transformative Leadership Programme for Democratic Change in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
